data.title: 'Both Sides of the Coin,' Says Top Investor About Amazon Stock
data.description:
It's been a middling period for investors in Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), with one-year gains in the high single digits. While that's better than being in the red, it's not all that impressive when compared to the performance of some of the other AI champs out there.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction AMZN bears now have this Tradr ETF

The company's ambitions haven't diminished through the years, as its planned $200 billion in 2026 capex concretely reflects. It's also likely one of the reasons that its share price hasn't been shooting upward, as the market isn't fully smitten with all the large outlays.

That hasn't stopped top investor Geoffrey Seiler, who calls Amazon one of his "favorite short- and long-term buys."

"Amazon is seeing accelerating cloud computing revenue growth and strong operating leverage in its e-commerce business," shares the 5-star investor, who is among the top 3% of stock pros covered by TipRanks and a writer for The Motley Fool.

Seiler appreciates the fact that the company's large capex has been a drag on share prices this year. And yet, he thinks things will work out in the end just fine.

The investor points to the company's history of heavy investments, positing that Amazon tends to emerge "much stronger" from these cycles. He predicts that the spending on AI infrastructure will lead to "accelerating revenue growth" for AWS.

Seiler notes that Amazon basically invented the infrastructure-as-a-service cloud industry, and it remains the market leader to this day. However, in some ways, the investor points out, the company's large reach has been conspiring against it.

"Because of AWS' sheer size, though, its growth rate has lagged behind its peers," he adds. He's also expecting the Q2 earnings to show that Amazon's growth is both "robust" and "sustainable," namechecking the company's partnership with Anthropic.

The flip side of a lukewarm share price is the "historically cheap" valuation at which AMZN is trading. Seiler notes that the company's forward price-to-earnings ratio is less than both Costco and Walmart.

"That makes Amazon a bargain stock to buy ahead of earnings and to hold for the long term," concludes Seiler. (To watch Seiler's track record, click here) 

Wall Street clearly has no objection. With 45 Buys and just a single Hold, AMZN storms out to a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $318.98 points to gains of 30%. (See AMZN stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Dow Jones Retreats as Oil Rallies Above $100 Per Barrel
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) opened Thursday in negative territory as rising tensions in the Middle East continue to drive oil prices higher. Brent crude (BZ) traded above $100 per barrel, marking the first time since late May.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The U.S.-Iran war continues to rage on and has now expanded to new fronts. In the early morning, Houthi forces, allies of Iran, said they had struck two Saudi tankers attempting to cross the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. The Houthis previously enforced a maritime blockade on Saudi ports, warning of violence if it was violated.

Oil Risk Grows as Trump Holds Iran Responsible

The Bab al-Mandeb Strait handles roughly 12% of global seaborne oil flows. That puts another energy chokepoint, in addition to the Strait of Hormuz, at risk.

In a Truth Social post, President Trump labeled the Houthis as a "Surrogate and/or Proxy of Iran." He also said the U.S. would hold Iran responsible if the Houthis choose to attack again. That comes as the conflict shows no signs of easing, with strikes against Iran reaching their 12th consecutive day.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Stock Futures Fall as Alphabet and Tesla's Earnings Spark AI Capex Concerns; Oil Prices Surge
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down on Thursday morning as the second-quarter results of tech giant Alphabet (GOOGL) and electric vehicle (EV) maker Tesla (TSLA) weighed on investor sentiment amid higher AI spending plans. Furthermore, a spike in oil prices added to investors' woes. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.93%, 0.66%, and 0.75%, respectively, at 8:25 a.m. EDT on July 23.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Oil prices surged after Tehran-backed Houthi militant group claimed attacks on two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea and President Donald Trump threatened to strike Iranian infrastructure. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was up 5.20% at $98.96 per barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) rose 4.91% to $90.92 per barrel.

During Wednesday's regular trading session, major U.S. stock indices closed lower ahead of Big Tech earnings and escalating geopolitical risks. The Nasdaq Composite, S&P 500, and Dow Jones fell 0.57%, 0.14%, and 0.01%, respectively, after all three had rallied in the previous session on a rebound in chip stocks.

In Thursday's pre-market trading, Alphabet and Tesla shares were down 5.1% and 7.3%, respectively. Alphabet raised its 2026 capital expenditure outlook to as much as $205 billion to expand its AI infrastructure. Meanwhile, Tesla outlined plans for higher capital spending as it continues investing in AI, Optimus robots, and Robotaxis.

In contrast, ServiceNow (NOW) stock rose more than 6% as the enterprise software company delivered market-beating Q2 earnings, easing AI disruption concerns. Also, Lockheed Martin (LMT) was up more than 5% after the defense company reported upbeat Q2 results.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: SpaceX Stock Price Forecast — Can Starship's Next Launch and Q2 Earnings Lift SPCX?
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) stock has two big catalysts ahead: today's Starship launch attempt and its second-quarter earnings report on Aug. 4. A strong earnings report could show the business is back on track. Meanwhile, a successful Starship launch could raise SpaceX's launch capacity and help win more business. With SPCX still down 28% from its debut, some investors may see the recent pullback as a buying opportunity.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

Why Starship Flight Matters to Investors

Starship Flight 13 is set to launch tonight, July 23, from SpaceX's Starbase in South Texas. This is the second try after the July 16 attempt was called off.

A successful launch would deploy 20 next-gen Starlink V3 satellites for the first time. The mission is important because Starship is key to SpaceX's future growth. In addition, it will support NASA's Artemis missions and future space-based data centers. A successful flight would also move SpaceX closer to the fast, low-cost launch system that many investors believe is critical to its long-term growth.

SPCX Stock Faces Its First Big Earnings Test

SpaceX will report its first quarterly earnings on Aug. 4, giving investors their first detailed look at the company's financial results since going public. The report will also trigger SpaceX's unusual lock-up period, allowing insiders and employees to sell shares earlier than the standard 180-day lock-up. Under the agreement, up to 911.5 million insider-held shares could become eligible for sale two trading days after the earnings release.

Is SPCX a Buy?

The bull case is that many insiders may choose not to sell. Long-term holders could keep their shares. Moreover, the lock-up expiration has been well known for months, and some investors believe the risk is already reflected in the stock price.

The bear case is that some insiders may take profits. Many bought their shares at much lower prices before the IPO, giving them a strong reason to sell once the lock-up ends. Heavy insider selling could weigh on SPCX in the short term.

What Is SPCX's Stock Price Target?

SPCX stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks based on 23 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell assigned after its IPO. The average price target for SpaceX is $243.81, implying an upside of over 100% from the current price.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Amazon Stock's CapEx Rise Is Coming – Don't Let It Overshadow Q2 Results, Says BofA
data.description:
CapEx is the defining theme of this earnings season. With Alphabet and Tesla having already released their Q2 results and both increasing their capital spending forecasts, investors will be keeping a close watch on how their Mag 7 peers' spending plans are shaping up.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

AMZO: built for a short position on AMZN

That will certainly be the case when Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) reports Q2 earnings next Thursday (July 30). The e-commerce giant has been committing substantial sums to data center infrastructure and is currently expected to spend ~$200 billion on CapEx this year.

Looking ahead to next week's readout, BofA's Justin Post, an analyst ranked among the top 2% on Wall Street, thinks that due to higher memory costs, Amazon could raise its 2026 capex outlook to $210 billion. If the market's response to the higher CapEx forecasts from Alphabet and Tesla is any indication, such an increase could weigh on the stock.

However, Post believes the results should also "highlight improving AI positioning."

Beyond headline revenue and GAAP operating income, Post will be focused on AWS growth, AWS margins, additional commentary regarding CapEx and the returns generated by AI investments, backlog expansion, and the Q3 outlook, as more challenging year-over-year comparisons for the retail business could create some pressure, although Post believes this could be partly offset by accelerating AWS growth and favorable pricing trends.

Amazon's Q2 guidance midpoint implies 17% year-over-year growth, unchanged from the first quarter, although Post sees potential upside based on his expectation of an AWS beat and industry data suggesting that e-commerce growth accelerated from Q1. "Ultimately," the 5-star analyst went on to say, "we believe the biggest driver of the stock continues to be expectations for the AI opportunity and returns on capex, so AWS revenue growth and margins are key focus areas."

He expects backlog to increase by $100 billion sequentially as a result of the Anthropic deal. For AWS, Wall Street expects Q2 revenue growth of 31% YoY, representing a 3-percentage-point acceleration from the first quarter. However, based on conversations with investors, Post believes expectations could be closer to 34% or higher, supported by robust backlog growth, a rising contribution from Anthropic, additional demand generated by OpenAI's inclusion on Bedrock, and third-party data.

On the margin front, consensus stands at 33.7%, down 400 basis points sequentially due to a seasonal increase in stock-based compensation, although that would still represent an 80-basis-point improvement vs. last year, with BofA forecasting 34%.

Naturally, Post identifies the biggest risk heading into the earnings release as the possibility that a higher CapEx guide could overshadow a strong revenue performance. However, he believes that concern could be eased by constructive commentary surrounding cloud demand, pricing, and adoption trends.

"We continue to see Amazon as a top AI beneficiary," Post summed up, reiterating a Buy rating and price objective of $310, implying shares will gain 27% over the next year. (To watch Post's track record, click here)

The Street's average target is only a little higher – at $318.98, the figure makes room for 12-month returns of 30%. On the rating front, based on a lopsided mix of 45 Buys and 1 Hold, the analyst consensus views this stock as a Strong Buy. (See AMZN stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Why Is Penny Stock Webuy Global (WBUY) Up Today?
data.description:
Webuy Global (WBUY) stock underwent a massive rally on Thursday after the travel solutions company announced new partnerships. The company said it will expand its global tourism network as it looks to benefit from China's growing inbound travel market. The company announced plans to join the Pacific Asia Travel Association (PATA) and take part in the China Inbound Tourism Alliance Conference.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Webuy Global said the moves support its long-term goal of building an AI-driven travel ecosystem for international visitors. China's inbound tourism market has gained momentum as visa rules have eased and global travel demand has improved. The company noted that its AI Smart Travel Card is designed to support travelers with trip planning, language help, live itinerary updates, and local travel guidance.

Webuy Global CEO Vincent Xue Bin said, "We believe China's inbound tourism market represents one of the most compelling long-term growth opportunities in global travel." He added that the company's PATA membership and conference participation are key steps in expanding its global network while investing in AI-powered travel services.

Looking ahead, Webuy Global plans to deepen partnerships with tourism boards, local governments, hotels, and other travel groups. The company believes those efforts will "deliver differentiated travel experiences while supporting the long-term development of China's inbound tourism sector."

How Did This News Affect WBUY Stock Today?

Webuy Global stock was up 95.25% alongside its expansion news, following a 1.55% rally yesterday. Even so, the shares have fallen 41.69% year-to-date and has dropped 89.49% over the past 12 months.

WBUY stock trading activity today saw some 100 million shares change hands. That's well above the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 84,000 shares.

Is WeBuy Global Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, traditional analyst coverage of WeBuy Global stock is lacking. Fortunately, TipRanks' AI analyst Spark has it covered. Using the xAI – Grok-3 model, Spark rates WBUY stock as Underperform with a $3 price target. It cites "significant challenges, primarily driven by poor financial performance with ongoing losses and negative cash flows" as reasons for this stance. (See WBUY Stock Forecast)
data.author: William White

data.title: Palo Alto Networks (PANW) CEO Calls OpenAI's Rogue AI Hack the 'Next Level of Cyber Incidents'
data.description:
Nikesh Arora, the CEO of cybersecurity firm Palo Alto Networks (PANW), is drawing attention after he called the hack into Hugging Face, an open-source AI platform, "the next level of cyber incidents."

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

His comments followed reports that two advanced AI models from ChatGPT creator OpenAI had breached Hugging Face during an internal cybersecurity test on July 22. The incident has renewed concerns about the growing cyber risks posed by powerful AI systems.

Arora Warns AI Threats Have Reached a New Level

The Palo Alto Networks CEO said OpenAI's Hugging Face hack shows that cyber threats are entering a new era, where AI systems can independently carry out complex attacks. He warned that security teams must prepare for more advanced AI-powered threats instead of relying only on traditional defenses.

Arora urged frontier AI developers to first use their own models to assess internal systems and code for security flaws before expanding tests. He said that approach could expose weaknesses before AI systems take advantage of them.

Arora also advised developers to deploy offensive and defensive AI agents together so security systems can detect attacks in real time. He added that tracking unusual AI inference activity and boosting guardrails will become even more important as more autonomous models emerge.

Hugging Face CEO Calls for Industry-Wide AI Safety

Arora's warning came after OpenAI disclosed details of the hacking incident. The firm said that its GPT-5.6 Sol and an unreleased model were being tested on ExploitGym, an internal benchmark for measuring offensive cyber skills, in a controlled space without internet. 

Instead of completing the test, the AI models broke out of the space by exploiting security flaws. It then reached the internet and breached Hugging Face to obtain the answers. OpenAI said the models generated more than 17,000 attack events before they were detected. It described the breach as an "unprecedented cyber incident" 

Meanwhile, OpenAI said it is strengthening the models' containment, monitoring, access controls, and evaluation practices. Hugging Face CEO Clément Delangue also stated that the incident highlights the need for industry-wide collaboration on AI safety.

Is Palo Alto a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, Palo Alto Networks (PANW) carries a Strong Buy consensus based on 41 analyst ratings over the past three months. Among them, 36 rate it a Buy, while 5 recommend Hold. The stock also has an average price target of $337.92, implying about 0.79% upside. (See PANW stock forecast)
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: ServiceNow Stock (NOW) Pops as Q2 Earnings Ease AI Disruption Concerns; Bulls Raise Price Targets
data.description:
ServiceNow (NOW) stock rose more than 5% in Thursday's pre-market trading as the enterprise software company's market-beating second-quarter earnings eased investor concerns about AI models disrupting traditional software businesses. Notably, several software stocks have been under pressure due to fears of a "SaaSpocalypse" – a term reflecting worries about a downturn in software-as-a-service (SaaS) businesses due to ⁠growing capabilities of AI startups like OpenAI and Anthropic.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

ServiceNow offers software to manage and automate workflows. The company has also expanded its presence in the cybersecurity market via the acquisitions of Veza and Armis.

Key Insights from ServiceNow's Q2 Earnings

ServiceNow reported a 24% growth in its Q2 2026 revenue to $3.99 billion. Notably, subscription revenue surged 24.5% to $3.88 billion, driven by strength in net new ACV (annual contract value) and a better-than-anticipated on-premise revenue mix due to solid U.S. Federal demand. The company's adjusted EPS (earnings per share) rose about 10% to $0.90. Analysts expected EPS of $0.86 on revenue of $3.93 billion.

The company is seeing strong demand for its AI Control Tower offering amid growing deployment of agentic AI. Importantly, ServiceNow AI crossed $1 billion in annual contract value in the second quarter.

ServiceNow ended Q2 2026 with a 21% growth in remaining performance obligations (RPOs) to $29 billion. CEO Bill McDermott attributed this growth to "longer customer commitments and skyrocketing demand from our partner ecosystem."

Looking ahead, ServiceNow raised its full-year subscription revenue guidance to $15.760 billion-$15.780 billion from the previous outlook of $15.735 billion-$15.775 billion, citing net new ACV strength.

Here's How Analysts Reacted to ServiceNow's Q2 Earnings

Following the Q2 print, Jefferies analyst Samad Samana increased his price target on ServiceNow stock to $140 from $135 and reiterated a Buy rating. The analyst highlighted the company's "healthy beats" on constant-currency cRPO (current RPO), constant-currency subscription revenue, and operating margin.

Samana also noted that while the strength in ServiceNow's cRPO and subscription revenue was driven by deal timing, the net new ACV outperformance contributed to upside in both metrics.

Also, Bernstein analyst Peter Weed raised his price target for ServiceNow stock to $248 from $236 and reiterated a Buy rating, saying that Q2 results provided "much-needed relief" compared to Q1 earnings on certain aspects. The 5-star analyst noted that while the first-quarter results raised concerns about NOW's beat-and-raise consistency, core demand, and inorganic growth, Q2 results saw a solid 230 basis points (constant currency) beat in subscription revenue.

Weed noted that management has a conservative outlook for the second half despite strong demand, which makes it confident about delivering a beat and raise.

Is NOW Stock a Buy or Sell?

Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on ServiceNow stock based on 26 Buys, two Holds, and one Sell. The average NOW stock price target of $140.65 indicates 47.3% upside potential.

These price targets/ratings could change as more analysts react to Q2 results.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: American Airlines Beat Expectations but AAL Stock Drops. Are Higher Jet Fuel Expenses Eating Profits?
data.description:
American Airlines (AAL) beat expectations for both sales and profit last quarter, yet its stock price dropped 4.7% to $14.10 before the opening bell on Thursday. The main reason for the drop is because rising fuel bills have forced AAL's management to lower their profit forecast for the year and warn investors of a weak third quarter.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

While the company reported adjusted second-quarter earnings of 15 cents per share on $16.74 billion in revenue, topping Wall Street goals, investors focused heavily on the lower guidance ahead.

AAL's Higher Fuel Prices Have Led to Lower Profit Outlook

Rising fuel bills keep hitting airlines across the market. Growing trouble between the U.S. and Iran has pushed crude oil prices (CM:CL) to six-week highs, making jet fuel much more expensive for airlines everywhere.

In response, American Airlines lowered its full-year 2026 earnings outlook to a range between a loss of 65 cents per share and a profit of 65 cents per share. This update replaces its prior, more optimistic forecast.

The third-quarter outlook looks similarly challenging. The airline expects a third-quarter loss between 70 cents and 10 cents per share, which fell far short of Wall Street estimates pointing to a profit of 26 cents per share.

AAL's Strong Sales Beat Gets Overshadowed

The weak future guidance overshadowed a solid second quarter. Total revenue grew 16% compared to last year, beating analyst expectations. Additionally, adjusted profit per share came in well ahead of the 3 cents per share that Wall Street projected.

Despite the quarter's strong sales, high fuel costs and debt remain clear challenges. In a report prior to American Airlines' earnings, 5-star Wells Fargo (WFC) analyst Christian Wetherbee maintained an Hold rating on the airline with a $17 price target.

Wetherbee pointed out that while American is "creating a more durable airline" thanks to its new Citi (C) credit card deal, its debt level remains a major hurdle. He explained that "elevated financial leverage leaves us on the sidelines despite our favorable industry outlook."

Is American Airlines a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on American Airlines stock (AAL) based on seven Buys, seven Holds, and one Sell rating assigned in the past three months. AAL's average 12-month stock price target of $19.23 per share implies 30% upside potential. (See the AAL stock forecast)
data.author: Annika Masrani
